commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a package-scoped direct media HTTP client cloned from `http.DefaultTransport`, sets a 10s response-header timeout, routes `downloadDirectBytes` through it, and adds focused timeout tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection rather than a live run. Current main routes read-only media downloads to `downloadDirectBytes`, which uses `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)` with no response-header-specific timeout, so a pre-header stall is only bounded by the caller context.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus maintainer judgment on the 10s response-header compatibility tradeoff; there is no narrow automated repair left in the current diff.

target.title: fix: bound direct media HTTP downloads
target.body:
## Summary

Bounds direct WhatsApp media downloads with an explicit HTTP client instead of `http.DefaultClient`.

The direct-media fallback already limits response body size and validates hashes/HMACs, but the transport path had no explicit dial, TLS handshake, response-header, idle timeout, or connection-pool bounds. A media endpoint that accepts a request but stalls before response headers could hold a direct media download until the caller context is canceled.

## What changed

- Add a package-scoped direct media HTTP client.
- Add a 10s dial timeout.
- Add a 10s TLS handshake timeout.
- Add a 10s response-header timeout.
- Add expect-continue, idle connection, and idle pool limits.
- Route `downloadDirectBytes` through the bounded client instead of `http.DefaultClient`.
- Preserve the existing caller/body download budget by intentionally leaving `http.Client.Timeout` unset.

## Security impact

This reduces denial-of-service risk in long-running sync/media workflows. A media server or network path that accepts a request but never returns headers should no longer pin the direct media download path indefinitely.

## Compatibility note after review

The earlier version used `http.Client.Timeout: 30 * time.Second`. ClawSweeper correctly flagged that as too broad because `http.Client.Timeout` covers the full request, including reading a valid response body. Direct media downloads can be large and are already governed by the caller context plus the existing media size cap.

This revision removes the total request timeout and uses transport phase bounds only. That keeps pre-header stalls bounded while preserving the existing command/caller context for legitimate response-body downloads.

## Behavior proof

### Dedicated client and no total body timeout

```text
=== RUN   TestNewDirectMediaHTTPClientBoundsPhasesWithoutTotalBodyTimeout
    media_timeout_test.go:50: direct media behavior: client.Timeout=0s, so valid response bodies keep the caller context budget
    media_timeout_test.go:51: direct media behavior: phase bounds active: TLSHandshakeTimeout=10s ResponseHeaderTimeout=10s ExpectContinueTimeout=1s IdleConnTimeout=30s MaxIdleConns=10 MaxIdleConnsPerHost=4
--- PASS: TestNewDirectMediaHTTPClientBoundsPhasesWithoutTotalBodyTimeout (0.00s)
```

This proves the compatibility fix from review: the client no longer has a total request timeout that can cap valid response body downloads. Only transport phases are bounded.

### `downloadDirectBytes` uses the dedicated direct media client

```text
=== RUN   TestDownloadDirectBytesUsesDedicatedHTTPClient
    media_timeout_test.go:66: direct media behavior: dedicated directMediaHTTPClient handled request host=example.test path=/voice.ogg origin_header_present=true referer_header_present=true
    media_timeout_test.go:89: direct media behavior: downloadDirectBytes used dedicated client and returned 11 bytes
--- PASS: TestDownloadDirectBytesUsesDedicatedHTTPClient (0.00s)
```

This proves `downloadDirectBytes` is routed through `directMediaHTTPClient`. If the implementation regresses back to `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)`, the injected transport is not called and this test fails.

### Pre-header stall is bounded

```text
=== RUN   TestDownloadDirectBytesFailsFastWhenServerStallsBeforeHeaders
    media_timeout_test.go:118: direct media behavior: pre-header stall failed in 50.573862ms with error "Get \"http://127.0.0.1:36037/voice.ogg\": net/http: timeout awaiting response headers"
--- PASS: TestDownloadDirectBytesFailsFastWhenServerStallsBeforeHeaders (0.05s)
```

This proves a server that accepts the request but does not return headers is bounded by `ResponseHeaderTimeout`.

### Slow body still succeeds after headers

```text
=== RUN   TestDownloadDirectBytesAllowsSlowBodyAfterHeaders
    media_timeout_test.go:152: direct media behavior: headers arrived before the 50ms ResponseHeaderTimeout, then slow body completed in 151.20685ms and returned 9 bytes
--- PASS: TestDownloadDirectBytesAllowsSlowBodyAfterHeaders (0.15s)
```

This directly addresses the review concern. `ResponseHeaderTimeout` bounds the pre-header phase, but it does not cap the response body after headers arrive.

## Validation

Focused behavior proof:

```text
PASS
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/wa   0.212s
```

Full repo validation:

```text
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/wa   0.215s
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/cmd/wacli     (cached)
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/app  (cached)
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/config       (cached)
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/fsutil       (cached)
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/linkpreview  (cached)
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/lock (cached)
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/out  (cached)
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/pathutil     (cached)
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/resolve      (cached)
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/sqliteutil   (cached)
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/store        (cached)
?       github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/store/storedb        [no test files]
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/syscontacts  (cached)
ok      github.com/openclaw/wacli/internal/wa   (cached)
```

## Current GitHub Actions status

Fork-triggered workflow runs still require maintainer approval before they execute. Local behavior proof and full local test validation are included above.

Current proof head: `9d3d9ab`.
